body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 71/2026
Made under: Wildlife Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 28, 2026

B.C. Reg. 71/2026, deposited April 28, 2026, under theWILDLIFE ACT[sections 6.4, 6.5, 108 and 109]. Ministerial Order M187/2026, dated April 28, 2026. I, Randene Neill, Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship, order that (a)	the Controlled Alien Species Regulation, B.C. Reg. 94/2009, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 1, and (b)	the Wildlife Act Commercial Activities Regulation, B.C. Reg. 338/82, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 2. — R. NEILL,Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship. 1	The Controlled Alien Species Regulation, B.C. Reg. 94/2009, is amended by adding the following section to Part 2: Transition – familyFelidae [cats] 6.1(1)	Despite sections 3 and 5.1 and section 1 (i) of Schedule 1, a person is not required to hold a permit to possess, ship or transport a prohibited species individual described in section 1 (i) of Schedule 1, except the following: (a)	a species of the subfamilyPantherinae[big cats such as lions, tigers and panthers]; (d)Lynx pardinus[Iberian lynx]. (2)	This section is repealed effective May 1, 2027. 2	Schedule 1 is amended by repealing section 1 (i), (j), (l) and (m) and substituting the following paragraph: (i)	all species of the familyFelidae[cats], except the following: (i)Puma concolor[cougar]that are native species; (ii)Lynx canadensis[lynx]that are native species; (iii)Lynx rufus[bobcat]that are native species; (iv)Felis catus[domestic cat];. 1	Section 3.04 of the Wildlife Act Commercial Activities Regulation, B.C. Reg. 338/82, is amended (a)	in subsection (1) (q) by striking out ", live box trap or egg trap," and substituting "or live box trap,", and (b)	by adding the following subsection:

minutes_briefs.title: BC bans possession of most exotic cats without a permit starting May 2027
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has amended its Controlled Alien Species Regulation to classify almost all cat species (family Felidae) as prohibited, with exceptions for native cougars, lynx, bobcats, and domestic cats. This means that as of May 1, 2027, anyone who owns, ships, or transports a non-exempt exotic cat (like a serval, caracal, or jungle cat) must have a permit. Big cats like lions and tigers are already covered by a separate rule. The change gives owners a transition period until May 1, 2027, to get a permit or find a new home for their animal.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own an exotic cat (other than a native cougar, lynx, bobcat, or domestic cat), apply for a permit before May 1, 2027.

key_dates.label: Exotic cat permit requirement takes effect in BC

minutes_briefs.source_body:
